title: guardrails-as-product (mcp --policy)
manager: The keynote demo becomes a product — an agent's tool surface gated by a certified machine, so "never charge twice without confirmation" is a proof, not a prompt you hope the model obeys. This is FSL securing the very interface that exposes FSL to AI: tool calls become events, legality becomes a current-state transition check, and every policy decision lands on the tape for audit. A certified policy machine is a guardrail that can't be talked out of its rules by clever phrasing.
cs: `mcp --policy guard.fsl`; tool calls = events, legality = current-state transition check; policy machines certified + lockfile-pinned; decisions land on the tape (auditable); template library (no-network-after-secret, approval-before-delete, tests-before-deploy).
